questions.question: State two types of establishment found in the commercial sector of the hospitality industry. [2]
questions.model_answer: Any two of: hotels, restaurants, cafes, fast-food outlets, pubs and bars, guesthouses, or event and conference venues - businesses that provide hospitality services in order to make a profit.

questions.question: Describe two ways in which staff can deal effectively with a customer complaint. [2]
questions.model_answer: Listen carefully and politely to the customer without interrupting, and apologise for the problem so the customer feels understood. Then take prompt action to put it right - for example offering a replacement, refund or discount - and, if needed, refer the matter to a manager, so the customer leaves satisfied.

questions.question: State two qualities of an effective team leader in a hospitality business. [2]
questions.model_answer: Any two of: good communication skills; the ability to motivate and support the team; fairness and approachability; good organisation and time management; the ability to make decisions and solve problems; and leading by example with a positive attitude.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by 'cross-contamination' and state one way to prevent it. [2]
questions.model_answer: Cross-contamination is the transfer of harmful bacteria from one food (usually raw food such as raw meat) to another food, or via hands, surfaces or equipment. It can be prevented by using separate, colour-coded chopping boards and utensils for raw and ready-to-eat foods, and by storing raw meat below ready-to-eat food in the fridge.

questions.question: State three main nutrients the body needs and give the function of each. [3]
questions.model_answer: Any three of: carbohydrates - provide the body's main source of energy; proteins - needed for growth and repair of body tissues; fats - provide energy and help insulate the body; vitamins - help the body work properly and resist illness; minerals (such as calcium and iron) - needed for healthy bones, teeth and blood. (Water and fibre are also essential for hydration and digestion.)
